description: Dame Amanda Milling was Government Whip (Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury) from 14 November 2023 to 5 July 2024. She was previously Minister for Asia at the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office from 16 September 2021 to 7 September 2022, and Minister for Asia and the Middle East from February 2022 to 7 September 2022. Amanda read Economics and Statistics at University College London, graduating in 1997. Before becoming a Member of Parliament, Amanda worked in market research, specialising in the financial services sector. Amanda was previously Co-Chairman of the Conservative Party and Minister without Portfolio at the Cabinet Office from 13 February 2020 to 15 September 2021. She also served as Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) from 28 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Assistant Government Whip from January 2018 to July 2019. Amanda was the Conservative MP for Cannock Chase from 2015 to 2024. Amanda sat on the Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Committee, Education, Skills and the Economy Sub-Committee before joining the Government. Amanda was born in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, close to the Cannock Chase constituency she represented.

details.body:
Dame Amanda Milling was Government Whip (Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury) from 14 November 2023 to 5 July 2024.

She was previously Minister for Asia at the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office from 16 September 2021 to 7 September 2022, and Minister for Asia and the Middle East from February 2022 to 7 September 2022.

Amanda read Economics and Statistics at University College London, graduating in 1997. Before becoming a Member of Parliament, Amanda worked in market research, specialising in the financial services sector.

Amanda was previously Co-Chairman of the Conservative Party and Minister without Portfolio at the Cabinet Office from 13 February 2020 to 15 September 2021. She also served as Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) from 28 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Assistant Government Whip from January 2018 to July 2019.

Amanda was the Conservative MP for Cannock Chase from 2015 to 2024. Amanda sat on the Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Committee, Education, Skills and the Economy Sub-Committee before joining the Government.

Amanda was born in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, close to the Cannock Chase constituency she represented.

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Dame Amanda Milling

person.title: The Rt Hon Dame Amanda Milling

details.body:
The Minister’s responsibilities include:

  • Middle East
  • East Asia (including Great Britain-China Centre)
  • Australia and New Zealand
  • Overseas Territories (including Falkland Islands)
  • British International Investment (BII)
  • economic cooperation and growth
  • science and technology
  • development
  • International Development Strategy and Official Development Assistance (ODA) allocations (until publication)
  • migration and de-mining
  • global health
  • COVID-19
  • stakeholder engagement (including Wilton Park and British Council)

person.title: The Rt Hon Dame Amanda Milling

details.body: Whips are MPs or Lords appointed by each party to help organise their party’s contribution to parliamentary business. They are responsible for making sure the maximum number of their party members vote, and vote the way their party wants.

role.title: Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip)
